terms.definition: A 3D shape with 6 equal square faces, 12 edges, and 8 vertices. All sides of a cube are the same length.
terms.exampleUsage: A dice used to play ludo is shaped like a cube.

terms.definition: A 3D shape with 6 rectangular faces, 12 edges, and 8 vertices. A cuboid looks like a box and its faces can be different sizes.
terms.exampleUsage: A brick and a tiffin box are shaped like cuboids.

terms.definition: A 3D shape with 2 flat circular faces on top and bottom and 1 curved surface joining them. It looks like a pipe or a tin can.
terms.exampleUsage: A glass of water and a steel tumbler are shaped like cylinders.

terms.definition: A 3D shape with 1 flat circular face at the bottom and a pointed tip (vertex) at the top. It has 1 curved surface.
terms.exampleUsage: An ice cream cone and a birthday party cap are cone-shaped.

terms.definition: A perfectly round 3D shape like a ball. It has no edges, no vertices, and only 1 curved surface.
terms.exampleUsage: A cricket ball, a marble, and a globe are all spheres.

terms.definition: A flat or curved surface of a 3D shape. A cube has 6 flat faces. A cylinder has 2 flat faces and 1 curved face.
terms.exampleUsage: Each face of a dice has a different number of dots on it.

terms.definition: A line where two faces of a 3D shape meet. A cube has 12 edges. A cylinder has 2 curved edges.
terms.exampleUsage: If you run your finger along the side of a matchbox, you are tracing an edge.

terms.definition: A corner point where edges meet on a 3D shape. The plural of vertex is vertices. A cube has 8 vertices.
terms.exampleUsage: A cone has just one vertex at its pointed tip.

terms.definition: A solid shape that has length, width, and height. You can hold it in your hand. Also called a three-dimensional shape.
terms.exampleUsage: A ball, a box, and a can are all 3D shapes found in our homes.

terms.definition: The space formed when two straight lines meet at a point. Angles can be small or large depending on how open they are.
terms.exampleUsage: The corner of your notebook forms a right angle.

terms.definition: An angle that looks like the corner of a square or the letter L. The corners of books, doors, and windows are right angles.
terms.exampleUsage: Each corner of a rectangle has a right angle.

terms.definition: A surface that is not flat but rounded. Spheres, cylinders, and cones all have curved surfaces.
terms.exampleUsage: The outside of a ball is one big curved surface.
